topic.name: Privacy and Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at safeguarding individual privacy rights and ensuring consumer protection in digital communications and financial transactions.

Document 1: Preserving American Privacy Act of 2013 - Amends the federal criminal code to require a governmental entity operating a public unmanned aircraft system to minimize the collection or disclosure of covered information.
Prohibits a government entity from operating a public unmanned aircraft system and collecting or disclosing covered information for a law enforcement purpose.
Document 2: Consumer Choice in Online Video Act - Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to direct the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to promulgate regulations requiring Internet service providers (ISPs) to provide plain language disclosures.
Prohibits ISPs from engaging in unfair methods of competition, or unfair or deceptive acts or practices.
Document 3: Consumer Right to Financial Privacy Act of 2015 - Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to revise the prohibition against the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)'s obtaining, from a covered person or service provider, any personally identifiable financial information about a consumer.
Prohibits the CFPB from requesting, accessing, collecting, using, retaining, or disclosing nonpublic personal information about a consumer unless certain conditions are met.
Document 4: Email Privacy Act - Amends the Electronic Communications Privacy Act of 1986 to prohibit a provider of remote computing service or electronic communication service to the public from knowingly divulging to any governmental entity the contents of any communication.
Requires a law enforcement agency to provide the customer a copy of the warrant and a notice that such information was requested by the government.
Document 5: International Communications Privacy Act - This bill amends the federal criminal code by allowing a governmental entity to require providers of electronic communication services to disclose the contents of communications in electronic storage regardless of where those communications are located.
The bill allows a governmental entity to obtain those communications only if certain conditions for obtaining the warrant are met.
Document 6: Electronic Communications Privacy Act Amendments Act of 2013 - Amends the Electronic Communications Privacy Act of 1986 to prohibit a provider of remote computing service or electronic communication service to the public from knowingly divulging to any governmental entity the contents of any communication.
Revises provisions under which the government may require, pursuant to a warrant, the disclosure by such a provider of the contents of such communications.
Document 9: Empowering Law Enforcement to Keep America Safe Act of 2016 - This bill amends the federal criminal code to expand the categories of subscriber transactional records that the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) may request.
The bill prohibits the FBI from requesting the contents of an electronic communication.
Document 10: Community Broadband Act of 2007 - Prohibits any state or local government statute, regulation, or other legal requirement from having the effect of prohibiting any public provider from providing advanced telecommunications capability.
Requires any public provider that regulates competing providers of advanced telecommunications capability or services to apply its ordinances, rules, and policies without discrimination.

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating financial practices to protect consumers and ensure fair access to financial products.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to direct the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to promote the goal of long-term price stability.
Directs the Board to clearly articulate its lender-of-last-resort policy.
Document 2: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor to exclude from the computation of debts the secured or unsecured portions of debts secured by the debtor's principal residence.
Requires the court to disallow a claim that is subject to any remedy for rescission under the Truth in Lending Act.
Document 3: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act to authorize the issuance of remotely created checks only if the consumer has designated in writing a person to issue them.
Empowers the CFPB to investigate violations of this Act.
Document 4: Directs the Director of the CFPB to establish a Small Business Advisory Board to advise and consult with the CFPB in the exercise of its functions.
Requires the Director to establish a Credit Union Advisory Council and a Community Bank Advisory Council.
Document 5: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit a creditor or mortgage broker from arranging a consumer credit transaction secured by the principal dwelling of a consumer without first verifying the consumer's reasonable ability to make the scheduled payments.
Prohibits prepayment penalties.
Document 6: Defines 'prepaid funds' as funds deposited through a prepaid program or made available from a card, code, or other device.
States that prepaid funds deposited by an agent or nominee in an insured depository institution shall satisfy the requirements of the primary exclusion from the definition of deposit broker.
Document 7: Requires federal financial regulatory agencies to assess other federal regulations before issuing a regulation or order.
Determines whether the agency's proposal is in conflict with, is inconsistent with, or is duplicative of other regulations.
Document 8: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to repeal Title II governing the bankruptcy of financial institutions.
Defines 'covered financial corporation' as one incorporated or organized under any federal or state law that is predominantly engaged in financial activities.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to repeal requirements for least-cost resolution of troubled insured depository institutions.
Repeals certain FDIC procedures for extension of time and mailed notification regarding determination of claims.
Document 10: Amends the Securities Exchange Act to direct the SEC to apply fiduciary duty to brokers and dealers providing investment advice to retail clients.
Requires the trier of fact to order any person who knowingly violated securities laws to reimburse an issuer for any bonus or other compensation received during the period of the violation.

topic.name: Economic and Trade Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing economic growth, facilitating trade, and providing regulatory frameworks for financial and trade practices.
